code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Mapping predicates to clause fragments (subject omitted)
    # Mapping predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholders will be filled with a cleaned object value.
    clause_map = {
        # Airport‑specific
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "second runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "third runway surface is {object}",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "fourth runway surface is {object}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "fifth runway surface is {object}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "has a third runway {object} feet long",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "has a fourth runway {object} feet long",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "has fifth runway number {object}",
        # General geographic / political
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "language": "speaks {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "leader": "has leader {object}",
        "leaderParty": "is led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {object}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {object}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {object}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {object}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {object}",
        "hubAirport": "uses hub airport {object}",
        "headquarter": "is headquartered at {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {object}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {object}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {object}",
        "nativeName": "is also known as {object}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {object}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {object}",
        "foundedBy": "was founded by {object}",
        # Aircraft / military (fallback handled later)
    }
    # Helper to strip surrounding quotes from literal objects
    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        return obj.strip('"').strip("'")

    # Group clauses by subject to avoid repeating the subject in each clause
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = clause_map.get(t.predicate)
        obj_clean = _clean(t.object)
        if tmpl:
            clause_body = tmpl.format(object=obj_clean)
        else:
            # generic fallback – turn predicate into readable phrase
            pred_readable = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
            clause_body = f"{pred_readable} {obj_clean}"
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(clause_body)

    # Build a phrase for each subject, joining its predicates
    subject_phrases = []
    for subj, bodies in subject_map.items():
        if len(bodies) == 1:
            phrase = f"{subj} {bodies[0]}"
        else:
            phrase = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(bodies[:-1]) + f", and {bodies[-1]}"
        subject_phrases.append(phrase)

    if not subject_phrases:
        return ""
    if len(subject_phrases) == 1:
        sentence = f"{subject_phrases[0]}."
    else:
        sentence = f"{', '.join(subject_phrases[:-1])}, and {subject_phrases[-1]}."
    # Ensure the first character is capitalised (handles cases where subject may start lower‑case)
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
